words.definition_vi: Dễ thay đổi tâm trạng, hay nổi nóng; hoặc (về máy móc) hay bị hỏng, không ổn định
words.definition_en: Prone to sudden changes of mood; unreliable or unpredictable in behavior or function
words.example_sentence: The temperamental artist refused to perform unless the lighting conditions were perfect, frustrating the entire production team.

words.definition_vi: Thẳng thắn, chân thành, không che giấu ý kiến hoặc cảm xúc
words.definition_en: Truthful and straightforward in expression; frank and honest without concealment
words.example_sentence: In her candid interview, the politician admitted her previous mistakes and outlined her vision for reform.

words.definition_vi: Hay cáu kỉnh, dễ bực bội, thường phàn nàn về những điều nhỏ nhặt
words.definition_en: Childishly sulky or bad-tempered; prone to complaining about trivial matters
words.example_sentence: The petulant teenager's constant complaints about dinner made family meals increasingly uncomfortable.

words.definition_vi: Thích ở với mọi người, yêu thích giao tiếp xã hội; (động vật) sống thành đàn
words.definition_en: Fond of being in company; enjoying the company of others; social and outgoing
words.example_sentence: Her gregarious nature made her the perfect host; she thrived on organizing social gatherings and bringing people together.

words.definition_vi: Chịu đựng khó khăn một cách bình tĩnh, không than phiền; không biểu lộ cảm xúc
words.definition_en: Enduring pain or hardship without complaint; showing little or no emotion
words.example_sentence: Despite losing his job, he maintained a stoic demeanor and immediately began searching for new opportunities.

words.definition_vi: Hung hăng, hay cãi cọ, thích gây sự cố; có thái độ đe dọa hoặc thù địch
words.definition_en: Eager or quick to argue, fight, or cause trouble; aggressively defiant
words.example_sentence: The truculent tone of his email suggested he was looking for a confrontation rather than a constructive dialogue.

words.definition_vi: Lạc quan, tin tưởng vào kết quả tốt; (cổ) có màu đỏ hoặc máu
words.definition_en: Optimistic or positive, especially in an unrealistic way; confident about the future
words.example_sentence: Despite mounting evidence of the project's failure, the manager remained sanguine about its eventual success.

words.definition_vi: Cẩn trọng, thận trọng, xem xét kỹ lưỡng trước khi hành động
words.definition_en: Wary and unwilling to take risks; careful to consider all circumstances and consequences
words.example_sentence: The circumspect investor studied market trends for months before committing to any major financial decisions.

words.definition_vi: Cay đắng, chế nhạo, sắc sảo (về lời nói hoặc cách phê bình); có vị chua
words.definition_en: Sharp and forthright, especially in a way that is hurtful or critical; bitter in taste or tone
words.example_sentence: The critic's acerbic review of the film was so scathing that it discouraged many potential viewers.

words.definition_vi: Tốt bụng, nhân hậu, không gây hại; (y học) không nguy hiểm
words.definition_en: Kind and gentle; not harmful or malignant; showing goodwill
words.example_sentence: The benign elderly woman spent her retirement volunteering at the local community center.

words.definition_vi: Thiếu tự tin, e thẹn, không dám khẳng định bản thân
words.definition_en: Lacking confidence in one's own abilities; shy or modest to an excessive degree
words.example_sentence: Despite her exceptional qualifications, her diffident nature prevented her from applying for the leadership position.

words.definition_vi: Cứng đầu, bướng bỉnh, không chịu thay đổi ý kiến hoặc hành động
words.definition_en: Stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion or course of action; difficult to manage or influence
words.example_sentence: His obstinate refusal to accept modern technology made it difficult for him to adapt to the digital workplace.

words.definition_vi: Cao thượng, rộng lượng, không tính toán nhỏ nhen; hào phóng và khoan dung
words.definition_en: Generous and forgiving, especially toward a rival or someone less powerful; noble in spirit
words.example_sentence: The magnanimous victor offered his defeated opponent a position in his new administration.

words.definition_vi: Ngắn gọn, tức tưởi, nói ít lời nhưng đầy ý nghĩa
words.definition_en: Using very few words; brief and concise in speech or writing
words.example_sentence: The CEO's laconic response to the journalist's question left everyone wanting more details.

words.definition_vi: Quan điểm lạc quan, kỳ vọng tích cực về tương lai
words.definition_en: An optimistic or hopeful perspective on future events or outcomes
words.example_sentence: Despite economic challenges, analysts maintain a sanguine outlook for the technology sector next year.

words.definition_vi: Khả năng phục hồi cảm xúc, vượt qua những khó khăn mà không bị tổn thương lâu dài
words.definition_en: The capacity to recover quickly from emotional difficulties and adapt to stress
words.example_sentence: Building emotional resilience in adolescents requires consistent support from family and mentors.

words.definition_vi: Hệ thống giá trị đạo đức cá nhân; khả năng phân biệt đúng sai
words.definition_en: A person's internal sense of right and wrong that guides their ethical decisions
words.example_sentence: The whistleblower's strong moral compass compelled her to expose the company's illegal practices.

words.definition_vi: Giả vờ mạnh mẽ, che giấu nỗi sợ hãi hoặc buồn bã; cố gắng trông bình tĩnh
words.definition_en: To appear courageous or confident despite feeling afraid, sad, or worried
words.example_sentence: After receiving the diagnosis, she put on a brave face for her children while privately struggling with fear.

words.word: wear one's heart on one's sleeve
words.definition_vi: Để lộ cảm xúc, không che giấu những gì mình cảm thấy; thẳng thắn về tình cảm
words.definition_en: To openly display one's emotions or feelings without trying to hide them
words.example_sentence: Unlike her reserved colleagues, she wears her heart on her sleeve, making her feelings immediately apparent to everyone.

words.definition_vi: Mất kiểm soát, nổi cơn giận, bộc phát cơn tức giận
words.definition_en: To become suddenly angry and lose emotional control
words.example_sentence: When the referee made that controversial call, the coach nearly lost his temper and was ejected from the game.

words.definition_vi: Chấp nhận, thích nghi với một tình huống khó khăn hoặc mất mát
words.definition_en: To accept and deal with something difficult or unpleasant; to reach an understanding about
words.example_sentence: It took her years to come to terms with her childhood trauma, but therapy eventually helped her heal.

questions.question_text: Which word best describes someone who is prone to sudden mood changes and unpredictable behavior?
questions.explanation: Temperamental có nghĩa là dễ thay đổi tâm trạng, hay nổi nóng. Benign = tốt bụng, Laconic = ngắn gọn, Circumspect = cẩn trọng. Chỉ A phù hợp với mô tả.

questions.question_text: The politician's _____ admission of her past mistakes earned her respect from voters who valued honesty.
questions.explanation: Candid = thẳng thắn, chân thành. Ngữ cảnh yêu cầu từ chỉ sự thẳng thắn trong việc thừa nhận lỗi lầm. Temperamental = hay nổi nóng, petulant = cáu kỉnh, stoic = bình tĩnh không phù hợp.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is closest in meaning to 'truculent'?
questions.option_c: Aggressively defiant and eager to argue
questions.explanation: Truculent = hung hăng, hay cãi cọ, thích gây sự cố. C là định nghĩa chính xác. A, B, D đều là antonyms hoặc không liên quan.

questions.question_text: Despite mounting evidence of failure, the manager's _____ attitude prevented her from acknowledging the project's problems.
questions.explanation: Sanguine = lạc quan (thường quá lạc quan). Ngữ cảnh chỉ ra rằng mặc dù có bằng chứng thất bại, người quản lý vẫn lạc quan. Circumspect = cẩn trọng, diffident = thiếu tự tin, acerbic = cay đắng không phù hợp.

questions.question_text: The _____ investor studied every detail before making any financial commitment, refusing to take unnecessary risks.
questions.explanation: Circumspect = cẩn trọng, xem xét kỹ lưỡng. Phù hợp với hành động 'studied every detail' và 'refusing to take unnecessary risks'. Gregarious = thích giao tiếp, obstinate = cứng đầu, petulant = cáu kỉnh không phù hợp.
Magnanimous = cao thượng, rộng lượng. Antonym là petty (nhỏ nhen) và vindictive (thù địch). A là định nghĩa của magnanimous, C và D không liên quan.

questions.question_text: The CEO's _____ response to the journalist's question—just three words—left everyone wanting more information.
questions.explanation: Laconic = ngắn gọn, tức tưởi. 'Just three words' là gợi ý rõ ràng cho từ này. Gregarious = thích giao tiếp, benign = tốt bụng, magnanimous = cao thượng không phù hợp.

questions.question_text: Which phrase means to openly display one's emotions without trying to hide them?
questions.option_b: Wear one's heart on one's sleeve
questions.explanation: Wear one's heart on one's sleeve = để lộ cảm xúc, không che giấu. A = giả vờ mạnh mẽ (ngược lại), C = chấp nhận, D = nổi giận. Chỉ B phù hợp.
